rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STEPHANIE CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, line 9, PETITIONER STEPHANIE CHU DEMURRER Off calendar for department 501. Because this case concerns real property, all motions other than those involving the trial date must be noticed to be heard in department 501. Moreover, while the motion is largely incomprehensible, it seems clear that it was not filed and served with the notice required by Code of Civil Procedure 1005. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELI GEORGOPOULOS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Compensation Of Expert Per Code Civ. Proc. Section 2034.470 And Requesting Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Order Setting Compensation Of Expert Per Code Civ. Proc. Section 2034.470 And Requesting Sanctions Granted. No opposition filed. The compensation payable by the City for Mr. Quintero's expert work is set at $550 per hour. The City is awarded $3,300 in reasonable monetary sanctions. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PRECILA BALABBO VS. CRICUT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT CRICUT, INC.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff must serve code-compliant responses without objections no later than January 30, 2025. Defendant is awarded reasonable monetary sanctions of $3,000, which must be paid by January 30, 2025. rulings.Case Title: GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC VS. BRADDOCK ROAD, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, line 1, DEFENDANTS BRADDOCK ROAD, LLC, SEAL HARBOR PARTNERS, LLC, FIRENZE GROUP, LLC, DAVID PERLSTEIN, SUNG MOON'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT Granted in part. The portions of the second cause of action in the second amended complaint seeking specific performance and injunctive relief are stricken. The portions of the first and sixth causes of action in the second amended complaint seeking specific performance and injunctive relief are not stricken. Plaintiff concedes that it may not seek specific performance and injunctive relief on its second cause of action. At this stage of the case, and assuming the allegations of the second amended complaint are true as is required on a pleading motion, it cannot be determined as a matter of law that the plaintiff is not entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief on its first and sixth causes of action. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STACY SAAL VS. GLYDWAYS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To Assert Affirmative Defense Of Counteroffer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, line 7, DEFENDANTS GLYDWAYS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MARK SEEGER, VINOD KHOSLA'S Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To Assert Affirmative Defense Of Counteroffer Granted. Defendants' proposed new affirmative defense is not barred by judicial estoppel. Plaintiff has not shown that it will suffer legal prejudice as a result of the new affirmative defense. California law liberally permits amendments to answers at any stage of the case. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BIKOFF VS. LIONTREE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Nicholas Tuostos Responses To Requests For Production, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN BIKOFF's Motion To Compel Nicholas Tuostos Responses To Requests For Production, Set One. Hearing required unless the parties agree that all issues raised by the motion have been resolved as a result of recently served discovery responses by defendants or otherwise. If the parties do not agree that all issues raised by the motion have been resolved, they are required to meet and confer prior to the case being called by the court in an effort to resolve all issues raised by the motion. If the parties are unable to reach a resolution by a meet and confer session prior to the hearing, at the hearing the first question the court will pose is what issue(s) raised by the motion remain unresolved. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HK).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Nicholas Tuostos Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JONATHAN BIKOFF's Motion To Compel Defendant Nicholas Tuostos Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One. Hearing required unless the parties agree that all issues raised by the motion have been resolved as a result of recently served discovery responses by defendants or otherwise. If the parties do not agree that all issues raised by the motion have been resolved, they are required to meet and confer prior to the case being called by the court in an effort to resolve all issues raised by the motion. If the parties are unable to reach a resolution by a meet and confer session prior to the hearing, at the hearing the first question the court will pose is what issue(s) raised by the motion remain unresolved. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HK).

rulings.Case Title: LUIS MURILLO PAVON VS. EMILY HOLLOWAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Response To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 4, DEFENDANT JON HOLLOWAY, EMILY HOLLOWAY, AMY HOLLOWAY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Response To Form Interrogatories. Denied. The motion was filed more than 45 days after service of the assertedly deficient discovery responses. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HK).

rulings.Case Title: ON24, INC. VS. LABROOTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT ON24, INC. DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT On24's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for violation of Business and Professions Code 17200 in the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend and its demurrer to all other causes of actions in the cross-complaint is overruled. Labroots concedes that its 17200 claim is invalid. All other claims in the cross-complaint are adequately alleged. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: WAYMO LLC VS. KONSTANTINE NIKKA-SHER PITERMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Third-Party Witness Milanendra Vikramsingh Piterman And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of David S. Killoran
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 6, PLAINTIFF WAYMO LLC's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Third-Party Witness Milanendra Vikramsingh Piterman And For Sanctions. Denied. Waymo's effort to obtain third party discovery from Ms. Piterman fails because there are currently no non-fictitiously named defendants as to whom Waymo may obtain discovery and Waymo's moving papers establish that Waymo is neither ignorant of the existence of Ms. Piterman nor of a potential claim that it might assert against her and thus Ms. Piterman is not, as of the filing of the motion, a proper Doe defendant. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HK).
